title: Southern sweet potato pie
subtitle: Spiced sweet potato custard in a flaky pastry shell
excerpt: Roasted sweet potato puréed with eggs, cream, warm spices, and brown sugar, baked in a blind-baked pastry shell. The Southern American counterpart to pumpkin pie, with a smoother, denser custard and a sweeter base flavour.
sourceNotes: Sweet potato pie is a staple of Southern American cooking with roots in the colonial era. Amelia Simmons, American Cookery (1796), the first American cookbook, includes recipes for potato pudding that use sweet potato as the filling for a pastry case. The recipe moved into the formal Southern kitchen tradition through the nineteenth century. It is closely related to pumpkin pie but predates the widespread use of canned pumpkin in American domestic cookery.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked and cooled. Thaw overnight in the fridge.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps refrigerated for 3 days. Serve at room temperature.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: 180°C conventional / 160°C fan; blind-bake pastry at 200°C first

content.text: The key to a smooth sweet potato pie is roasting the sweet potato rather than boiling it: roasting concentrates the flavour and drives off water, giving a naturally sweeter, less watery purée. Boiled sweet potato can make the custard loose and bland. After roasting, the flesh is scooped out, beaten smooth, and mixed with the remaining filling ingredients. The texture of the finished pie should be dense and smooth, not porous or wet.

attrs.prepNote: about 2 medium sweet potatoes, roasted whole

content.text: Roast the sweet potato and blind bake the shell

Heat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan). Pierce the {{sweet-potato}} several times with a fork, place on a baking tray, and roast for 45-55 minutes until very soft and the flesh yields easily to a skewer. Meanwhile, make the shortcrust pastry from 200 g {{plain-flour}}, 100 g cold {{unsalted-butter}}, {{fine-salt}}, and 2-3 tbsp cold water. Chill for 30 minutes, then line a 23 cm pie dish, trim, and chill again. Blind bake with baking paper and beans for 15 minutes, remove the beans and paper, and bake 5 more minutes.

Reduce the oven to 180°C (160°C fan). Scoop the sweet potato flesh into a bowl (discard the skins) and beat smooth with an electric hand mixer. Add the melted {{unsalted-butter}}, {{soft-brown-sugar}}, {{whole-eggs}}, {{double-cream}}, {{ground-cinnamon}}, {{ground-ginger}}, {{ground-nutmeg}}, and {{vanilla-extract}}. Beat until smooth and well combined. Pour into the warm shell and bake for 40-45 minutes until the edges are set and the centre trembles slightly. Cool for at least 1 hour before slicing.
